welcome.title: Welcome to MetaMates
welcome.introNoWorkspace: Built for **creators, knowledge workers, and lifelong learners**: you have a steady stream of ideas but don’t want to organize them upfront. MetaMates lets you **capture first, process later**—vault on the left, editor in the center, **engine on the right as your daily starting point**. Choose a workspace folder to begin.
welcome.introReady: Your inspiration vault is ready. **Each day, start on the right—pick an AI, send a message, or run a command.** The engine reads Markdown in this folder and writes results back. The center editor is for reviewing and tweaking, not the only entry point.
welcome.stepSetup1: On the left, click **Open workspace** and choose a folder for your ideas (diary, inbox, and more are created on first run)
welcome.stepSetup2: On the right, **connect an AI assistant**—that’s the main entry; you don’t need a long note first
welcome.stepSetup3: Say “help me plan today” or tap **`/today`** and watch the engine write the plan to a file
welcome.stepReady1: On the right, **pick an AI and describe what you want now** (triage inbox, plan the day, upgrade a spark…)
welcome.stepReady2: Try **`/today`** for a daily plan, **`/graduate`** to promote a fragment to a permanent note, or **`/intel`** to clip a link
welcome.stepReady3: Open results in the center editor; use the **calendar** on the left for today’s journal, or **`Ctrl+P`** to create or import
welcome.stepAuth1: When the panel shows **sign-in required**, follow the prompt to authenticate your assistant
welcome.stepAuth2: After sign-in, send **`/today`** or any message to confirm the engine can read and write your vault
welcome.stepAuth3: You can also switch or install another assistant under **Settings → AI** (top right)
welcome.stepNoAgent1: No AI assistant detected—**without a CLI, MetaMates can only store, not transform**
welcome.stepNoAgent2: Click **Install AI assistant** in the right Agent panel, or open **Settings → AI** to install Gemini, Claude, or CodeBuddy
welcome.stepNoAgent3: Once connected, chat in the right panel or use slash commands
welcome.stepConnecting1: Connecting the thinking engine—usually a few seconds
welcome.stepConnecting3: Use the **microphone** next to the input for voice (mic permission required; Windows often needs network)
welcome.agentNeedsAuth: **Sign-in required.** Complete authentication in the panel on the right before the engine can read and write your vault.
welcome.agentReady: **Engine is ready.** Chat directly or use `/today`, `/graduate`, `/intel`, and more—results land in your notes.
welcome.agentNoAgent: No enabled AI assistant detected. **Without a CLI, MetaMates is vault-only**—click Install AI assistant on the right, or open **Settings → AI**.
welcome.commandsTitle: Common commands (read vault → process → write back)
welcome.commandsBody:
| Command | When to use it |
|---------|----------------|
| `/today` | Morning or start of work: pull context from the vault, generate today’s plan |
| `/closeday` | End of day: review today, set up tomorrow |
| `/graduate` | When an inbox fragment deserves to become a permanent note |
| `/intel` | Clip an article or document into the vault as intelligence |
| `/context` | Before a project session: let AI read related notes first |

See **?** (top right) for the full list.
welcome.tipsBody:
- **`Ctrl+P`** Command palette (new note, import intelligence, …)
- **`Ctrl+Shift+F`** Search across your vault
- **`@`** Reference a file in chat so the engine has context
- **`[[note name]]`** Link notes together
- **Microphone** on the right for voice input (mic permission; Windows desktop often needs network)

emptyState.footerHint: Output lands in your vault on the left and opens here for review and tweaks.

questions.noWorkspace: Which folder should hold your ideas?
questions.noAgent: Your vault is ready—connect an AI assistant to process these notes?
questions.authRequired: Your assistant isn’t signed in yet—authenticate so the engine can read your vault?
questions.newUser: What first: today’s plan, triage inbox, or upgrade a spark?
questions.morningNoPlan: Good morning—no PLAN yet. Pull today’s plan from your vault?
questions.morningNoPlanGentle: You’re ready to start, but today still has no clear handle. Want me to sketch a PLAN first?
questions.inboxGraduate: {{count}} fragments in Inbox—upgrade a few into permanent notes?
questions.inboxGraduateTheme: These fragments still haven’t collapsed into a theme. Want to first spot which ones are really about the same problem?
questions.inboxGraduateGentle: You’ve been collecting a lot lately. Maybe what you need now is synthesis, not another input. Want to do one pass?
questions.continueRecent: Keep shaping “{{name}}”, or ask the engine for a fresh angle?
questions.continueRecentDecision: You keep returning to “{{name}}”. Do you need to keep writing, or first name what’s actually blocking it?
questions.eveningCloseDay: Wrapping up—review today and set a hook for tomorrow?
questions.eveningCloseDayGentle: You moved things forward today. What may matter most now is not more input, but one line that hands tomorrow a clean start.
questions.eveningNoNote: No journal entry today—capture a few thoughts before you stop?
questions.eveningNoNoteGentle: Today wasn’t empty, it just hasn’t been captured yet. Want to save the one thing that matters most before you stop?
questions.reviewPlan: Today’s plan is in the vault—check progress against it?
questions.reviewPlanTension: The plan exists. The real question may not be what to do, but which important thing still hasn’t moved.
questions.afternoonNoPlan: Afternoon already—generate today’s plan from the vault now?
questions.afternoonNoPlanFocus: You’ve probably already started moving, but without a clear handle. Want to pin down the few things worth focusing on?
questions.planOpenTasks: “{{task}}” in your PLAN is still open—is it blocked by time, or by uncertainty whether it matters?
questions.planOpenTasksTension: {{count}} items unchecked in your plan. Is today stuck on execution, or on not knowing what comes first?
questions.planOpenTasksGentle: You already have a PLAN for today. Want to move “{{task}}” first and give the day a clear start?
questions.scheduleToday: {{count}} events on the calendar—before “{{event}}”, what’s the one thing worth pushing forward?
questions.scheduleTodayTension: The calendar is full but PLAN items remain open. Are you chasing the schedule, or what actually matters?
questions.ideasBrewing: “{{report}}” has ideas still in motion—which one deserves one real step today?
questions.ideasBrewingFocus: You’ve been brewing new ideas lately. Do you need more capture, or one idea pushed past the decision line?
questions.welcomeBack: What’s next: write, organize, or one sentence to the engine?
questions.welcomeBackFocus: You’re back. Maybe the problem isn’t a lack of options, but a lack of one clear focal point. Want to pick it first?

prefill.noWorkspace: I want to open a workspace folder for my notes
prefill.noAgent: Help me detect and connect an available AI assistant
prefill.authRequired: Help me complete AI assistant sign-in
prefill.newUser: I’m new—run /today to create my first daily plan from the vault
prefill.morningNoPlan: Please run /today and generate today’s plan from my vault context
prefill.inboxGraduate: Review my inbox and run /graduate on notes worth keeping
prefill.continueRecent: I want to continue improving “{{name}}”—read related notes and advise
prefill.eveningCloseDay: Please run /closeday to review today based on my notes and plan
prefill.eveningNoNote: Help me capture today’s journal highlights and whether to update the plan
prefill.reviewPlan: Read today’s PLAN and tell me what still needs attention
prefill.afternoonNoPlan: Run /today now based on what’s in my vault
prefill.planOpenTasks: Read today’s PLAN and help me decide if “{{task}}” should be my priority right now
prefill.scheduleToday: Given today’s calendar and PLAN, what’s the one thing I should push forward now?
prefill.ideasBrewing: Read “{{report}}” and related notes—pick one idea worth acting on today
prefill.welcomeBack: Based on my recent notes, what’s the one best thing to do next?
